checks.finding: T-001 Risk: high is supported by its Goal, Scope, and Risk Rationale: it changes mandatory review-input integrity and preserves the human/signed-sudo approval boundary required by REQ-016 and REQ-017, an access-control-sensitive workflow surface.

T-001 Done When explicitly requires Red→Green logs under specs/bootstrap-interviewer-enhancement/verification/ and a distinct independent reviewer verdict in reports/implementation/bootstrap-interviewer-enhancement/T-001.md.

tasks.md T-001 is oversized: its Scope combines more than three distinct implementation areas—layer templates and question bank, visualization documentation, Bash/PowerShell checker modes, review manifests/prechecks/prompts, regression fixtures, and synchronized release metadata. Planned Files likewise spans two plugins, catalogs/manifests, CHANGELOG.md, and tests. This cannot reasonably remain one coherent focused-session unit and risks partial integration and unverifiable rollback; task sizing is substantively reviewer-owned rather than a precheck-owned format issue.

T-001 maps REQ-006 through REQ-017, and acceptance-tests.md supplies associated error and edge paths including existing-file preservation, missing/tampered/substituted review inputs, invalid traversal-like slugs, malformed traceability values, LITE exclusion, legacy compatibility, no visual input, and Draft-task rejection.

No standalone test task with a declared unit, integration, end-to-end, or acceptance type appears in tasks.md; T-001 is a functional implementation task whose planned TEST-001 through TEST-019 evidence is mapped in acceptance-tests.md.

T-001 includes an explicit Rollback field covering templates, paired scripts, review-loop manifests, tests, and synchronized metadata, and its Done When requires rollback to be documented and exercised against an isolated fixture.
Only T-001 is defined, so no inter-task file or purpose overlap exists.
T-001 declares Blockers: None and no other task exists, so there is no missing or spurious inter-task dependency.
SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope.
